title: Scoop: How the left plans to start winning Democratic primaries again

title: Are US soldiers really 'refusing orders' to fight in Iran war?
description: More than two weeks after the US and Israel launched military strikes on Iran, a rumour has sprung up that American soldiers are refusing, en masse, to fight. There is no evidence for this. However, the number of conscientious objectors in the US military is on the rise, according to the NGO Center on Conscience & War.

title: Will the Jalisco cartel's new US-born leader complicate Trump's war on drugs?
description: PRESS REVIEW – Thursday, January 19: We look at why Russian oil is the big winner from the US and Israel's war in Iran. Also, the New York Times reveals sexual assault and rape allegations against the late US civil rights leader Cesar Chavez. In Mexico, the new leader of the Jalisco New Generation Cartel is a US-born citizen, which will complicate efforts to eradicate him. Plus: researchers reveal the science behind bad jokes in the workplace.

title: Greek animal airlift evacuates pets and owners from Middle East war
description: A flight carrying dozens of cats, dogs and their owners landed in Athens on Wednesday as part of a successful government effort to evacuate pets from the war in the Middle East. Greece's interior and foreign ministry worked together for days to coordinate the operation to repatriate Greek nationals without forcing them to leave their pets behind.

title: Senegal stripped of AFCON title: CAF ruling sets a 'very dangerous' precedent
description: Following the unprecedented decision to strip Senegal of the Africa Cup of Nations title and hand it to Morocco, Mark Owen is pleased to welcome Eurosport's Ruben Slagter. Not only is our guest troubled by the decision itself, but also the opacity of its timing and communication: the delayed ruling, coupled with its quiet release during a major European fixture, the Champions League, raises questions about institutional transparency and intent.

title: Higher energy prices might eat your tax refund, economists say
description: What the taxman giveth, the energy shock taketh away. title: USS Gerald R. Ford ‘operational’ after laundry fire injures sailors
description:
A fire broke out in the main laundry area of the USS Gerald R. Ford (CVN 78) while the aircraft carrier operated in the Red Sea as part of Operation Epic Fury, according to a statement from the US Naval Forces Central Command/US 5th Fleet.
The post USS Gerald R. Ford operational after laundry fire injures sailors appeared first on Naval Technology.

title: Analysis: Naim Qassem says Hezbollah will continue fighting Israel, frames war as defense of Lebanon
description:
Hezbollah Secretary-General Naim Qassem gave his second speech regarding the renewed war with Israel, once again arguing that his group was the victim of aggression and not the aggressor. He said that Hezbollah is fighting a war of self-defense on behalf of Lebanon and stressed that it would continue fighting until it secures adequate concessions from Israel.
The post Analysis: Naim Qassem says Hezbollah will continue fighting Israel, frames war

title: Dozens of airstrikes target Iran-backed militias across Iraq
description:
The unclaimed airstrikes, likely conducted by the United States as part of US-Israeli operations against the Islamic Republic of Iran, have targeted several Tehran-allied militias in Iraq and reportedly caused significant casualties. The US has not commented on these operations, which include over 30 reported strikes on the groups, who continue to attack US forces in the region. 
The post Dozens of airstrikes target Iran-backed militias across Ir

title: Iran-backed Iraqi militias claim downing of US aircraft, US says not due to ‘hostile fire’
description:
The Islamic Resistance in Iraq, a front organization for Iranian-backed militias, claimed to have shot down a US Air Force KC-135 Stratotanker over western Iraq but provided no evidence. US Central Command denied that the aircraft was shot down, and reports indicated that it had collided with another US warplane. 
The post Iran-backed Iraqi militias claim downing of US aircraft, US says not due to ‘hostile fire’ appeared first on FDDs Long War Jo

title: International shipping 'chaos' as Strait of Hormuz closure sends ripple effects across the globe
description: Around 90% of global goods are transported by sea. And while just approximately 5% of that travels directly through the Strait of Hormuz, Iran's effective closure of the key passage is having a ripple effect, disrupting trade routes world wide. FRANCE 24's Sharon Gaffney speaks with Ebru Surucu-Balci, Assistant Professor in Circular Supply Chains at the School of Management at the University of Bradford.

title: Qatar orders departure of two Iranian diplomats over gas facility attack
description: Qatar's foreign ministry on Wednesday ordered Iran's military and security attaches along with their staff to leave the country within 24 hours, following an attack on its massive natural gas facility. FRANCE 24's Hoda Abdel-Hamid reports from Doha.

title: Why Hungary's Viktor Orban is vilifying Ukraine before crucial elections
description: Weeks before crucial parliamentary elections, Hungarian Prime Minister Viktor Orban’s campaign has characterised his main rival as a stooge of Kyiv and Brussels. With his party trailing in the polls, the nationalist leader has sought to escalate tensions between Hungary and Ukraine, alleging that Kyiv and its European allies are conspiring to drag Hungary into war with Russia.

title: Are US soldiers really "refusing orders" to fight in Iran war?
description: More than two weeks after the US and Israel launched military strikes on Iran, a rumour has sprung up that American soldiers are refusing, en masse, to fight. There is no evidence for it. However, the number of conscientious objectors in the US military is on the rise, according to the Centre on Conscience and War NGO.

title: Liverpool thrash Galatasaray 4-0 to reach Champions League quarterfinals
description: Liverpool book quarterfinal with champions Paris Saint-Germain after dismantling Turkish giants Galatasaray at Anfield.

title: 10 major Iranian naval targets struck in Operation Epic Fury
description:
President Donald Trump has identified the Iranian Navy as a major target in the US campaign against the Islamic Republic of Iran. US forces have struck a range of port infrastructure and key vessels to degrade its capability, destroying more than 100 vessels by March 16.

description:

The eliminations add to the growing list of top Iranian officials who have been killed by Israeli and American strikes since the conflict began on February 28.

title: Purported Iran-backed group claims responsibility for attacks in Belgium and Greece
description:
A previously unknown organization called the Islamic Movement of the Companions of the Right claimed responsibility for an explosion that targeted a synagogue in Liège, Belgium, and another unconfirmed attack at an unknown location in Greece this week. It is unclear if the organization is authentic, but recent Iranian threats aimed at European countries over the war could indicate a connection to the Islamic Republic.

title: Senegal calls for inquiry after being stripped of AFCON title
description: In tonight's edition, Senegal is demanding a corruption investigation, after the Confederation of African Football stripped the Lions of Teranga of their 2025 Africa Cup of Nations title. Also, Nigerian President Bola Tinubu meets King Charles and the royal family at Windsor Castle for a 2-day visit. And we learn more about "the right of salt" -- an age-old custom in North Africa honouring women during Ramadan.

title: Israel kills Iran’s intel chief as both sides ratchet up attacks on energy facilities
description: Iran suffered major blows on Wednesday with the killing of its intelligence minister, Esmail Khatib, in an Israeli strike and an attack on the South Pars natural gas field, one of the world's largest gas resources and a pillar of the country's energy supplies. Tehran vowed retaliation for the attacks, targeting facilities in Saudi Arabia, Qatar, Oman, Kuwait, Bahrain and the UAE.

title: US seeks to curb soaring petrol prices
description: The Trump administration is taking steps to curb rising energy costs, as it continues to wage war in the Middle East. The US is easing sanctions on Venezuela, and suspending a century-old shipping law, in an effort to get more oil supplies into the global market. title: 'Acute risk' that Iraq could become once again victim of internal conflict as Iran war progresses
description: Speaking with FRANCE 24's Yinka Oyetade, Zaid Al-Ali, Visiting Fellow at Princeton University and Former UN Advisor in Iraq, explains that until the war in Iran broke out, Iraq had been able to "straddle between US-aligned interests and Iranian-aligned interests" but there's an "acute risk" that it will become the victim of internal conflict once again as the war progresses.

title: Iran did not try to rebuild enrichment after June 2025 attack, US intel chief says
description: Iran made no effort to rebuild uranium enrichment after its capabilities were destroyed in a June 2025 US-Israeli attack, US intelligence chief Tulsi Gabbard testified Wednesday, contradicting President Donald Trump's justifications for his ongoing war. FRANCE 24's Fraser Jackson reports from Washington.

title: Kharkiv's opera and ballet theatre performances continue despite war
description: Ukraine’s second-largest city, Kharkiv, is just 30km from Russia and suffers almost daily bombardment from across the border. But as the years of war have gone by, the city’s cultural institutions have adapted in order to keep working despite the dangers. description: The assessment in the intelligence agencies’ annual report comes as Beijing has stepped up pressure on Taiwan with frequent military drills.

title: Iran war may force US to shift missile defenses from South Korea, Seoul says
description: The U.S. Army operates only a small number of THAAD batteries across the world, meaning a deployment can affect multiple theaters.

title: US has destroyed entire class of Iranian warships, CENTCOM commander says
description: The U.S. had struck more than 60 Iranian ships, according to U.S. Central Command Commander Adm. Brad Cooper.

title: Amid US military actions, White House struggles to explain how Iran war will end
description: Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth on Tuesday told reporters it’s up to Trump “whether it’s the beginning, the middle or the end” of the war.

title: ‘Radiant’ mother from Kentucky was among 6 US service members killed in air crash in Iraq
description: The KC-135 was supporting operations against Iran on Thursday in “friendly” airspace when an unspecified incident involving another aircraft occurred.

title: Pentagon confirms identity of soldier killed in Kuwait
description: The Pentagon previously released the soldier's name but said a medical examiner had to confirm his identity.

title: 'Decapitation' strategy: Israel seeks to destroy Iran command and control
description: Israel said on Wednesday its forces had killed another top Iranian official, Intelligence Minister Esmail Khatib, and said its military was authorised to kill any senior figure of the Islamic republic in its sights. The announcement, the day after Iranian security chief Ali Larijani was confirmed killed in an Israeli strike, is part of a longstanding strategy by Israel to target its enemy's leaders. FRANCE 24's International Affairs Editor Kethev

title: 'Not a one-person regime': Why Iran's Islamic Republic is so hard to topple
description: Since the killing of Iran's Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei on February 28, US and Israeli strikes have decimated the regime's top ranks, from the commander of the Revolutionary Guards, to the country's security and intelligence chiefs. But even as the heads of the Iranian leadership roll, regime change remains unlikely anytime soon. FRANCE 24 spoke to Middle East expert Filippo Dionigi to understand why.

title: Elisa Catalano Ewers: Israeli leadership moves to destabilise regime’s leadership structure
description: Delano D'Souza is pleased to welcome Elisa Catalano Ewers, Senior Fellow for Middle East Studies at the Council on Foreign Relations. Our guest illustrates how Israeli envisions destroying the Iranian regime from within. Their approach is not one of immediate collapse, but of calculated and sustained pressure. Ms. Catalano Ewers finds many external assumptions deeply flawed, particularly the expectation that the Iranian population can mobilise un

title: Soler i Lecha: Foreign policy of Pedro Sánchez 'widely supported by the Spanish population'
description: As Ukraine's President Volodymyr Zelensky meets with European leaders, including Spanish Prime Minister Pedro Sanchez, Eve Irvine is pleased to welcome Eduard Soler i Lecha, Associate Professor of International Relations at the Universitat Autònoma de Barcelona and Senior Research Associate at IBEI. According to Mr. Soler i Lecha, Spain's current foreign policy is deeply strategic. Our guest illustrates that what we are witnessing under Pedro Sán

title: Could the Iran war trigger a global recession?
description: Energy prices are surging as the Iran war disrupts supply, raising risks for the US, China and Europe.

title: Who will answer for the Iranian schoolchildren killed in Minab?
description: An investigation finds the Minab school strike may have been deliberate. Who can hold the US accountable?

title: Iranian women’s football team return to Iran
description: The Iranian national womens football team has returned to Iran, including several players who withdrew asylum requests.

title: LIVE: Liverpool vs Galatasaray – Champions League last 16
description: Follow the build-up, analysis and live text commentary of the game as Liverpool host the Turkish champions at Anfield.

title: Gunshots disperse crowd during Persian New Year festivities in Tehran
description: A crowd celebrating Chaharshanbe Suri in Tehran were forced to flee after gunshots disrupted their event.

title: Pakistan and Afghanistan agree to temporary Eid al-Fitr ‘pause’ in conflict
description: Pakistani minister says five-day pause, to begin at midnight Thursday, requested by Saudi Arabia, Qatar and Turkiye.

title: How Los Angeles’s Iranian diaspora is confronting the US war on Iran
description: Residents are divided between supporting and opposing the US and Israeli war against Iran, amid fears for loved ones.

title: Air strike hit Kabul rehab centre as patients ate dinner, survivor tells BBC
description: The exact number killed in Monday night's strike by Pakistan is still not known, but is feared to be in the hundreds.

title: King Charles greets Nigerian president in Windsor sunshine
description: The King and President Tinubu will give speeches later at the state banquet, to be attended by political leaders and celebrities.

title: Retired Belgian Diplomat, 93, Faces Trial Over 1961 Killing of Congolese Leader
description: A Brussels court ruled that Étienne Davignon must stand trial for the murder of Patrice Lumumba, the first prime minister of the Democratic Republic of Congo.
